name: Taiga PM: rootless Podman Compose
description: Rootless Podman Compose stack for Taiga with nginx front door—secrets, volume layout, and TLS posture documented so multi-service PM software stays maintainable and minimally exposed.
longDescription: Infrastructure defined in version control for running Taiga without a Docker daemon: Podman Compose definitions, nginx as the HTTP entry (port 8080 by default), and guidance for WSL2 versus native Linux rootless setups. The stack separates HTTP ingress, application workers, PostgreSQL, and RabbitMQ with explicit volume mounts so permissions and backups are predictable. Deployment flow: copy `.env.example`, create persistent data directories, run `podman compose up`, create Django superusers, and optionally load default templates. Shell scripts for backup and restore ship with the stack.
features: Single HTTP entry via nginx reverse proxy
Rootless-first documentation for WSL2 and Linux servers
Data volume layout for postgres, media, static assets, and messaging
Guidance to avoid /mnt/c performance traps on WSL2
highlights: Rootless Podman fluency for long-running collaboration software—reduced daemon attack surface
Single nginx entry point simplifies TLS termination and access rules
Operational scripts for backup discipline and documented secret generation (SECRET_KEY, DB, RabbitMQ)
challenges: Taiga’s multi-service footprint is sensitive to volume permissions and async workers.
solutions: Document directory prep, env secrets (SECRET_KEY, DB passwords, Rabbit creds), and compose runbooks for first boot.
security: Generate strong secrets for SECRET_KEY, database, and RabbitMQ users
Do not expose port 8080 broadly without TLS termination
